Title: A system genetics analysis uncovers the regulatory variants controlling drought response in wheat

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase (KCS), condensing enzyme of very-long-chain fatty acid elongase
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fielder; Xinong511; Jimai22; Chinese Spring; Yanfu188; Ning9940
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cuticular wax biosynthesis; very-long-chain fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VLCFA elongation module leading to alkane and fatty acid wax components
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated very-long-chain 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates; contributes to C28 fatty acids and C31/C33 alkanes accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensation step of very-long-chain fatty acid elongation, extending acyl-CoA chains used for cuticular wax formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax components, especially alkanes and fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces non-stomatal water loss and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and directly determines wax component chain elongation for target wax products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a rate-limiting enzyme in wax biosynthesis by paper context and cited knowledge
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in Hap2/In-686 genotypes than Hap1/Del-686 under both well-watered and drought stress conditions; drought responsive
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced/associated with drought tolerance; higher transcript levels positively correlate with seedling survival rate under both well-watered and drought stress conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native TaKCS3 promoter haplotypes In-686 and Del-686 in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/TWAS/eQTL integration; haplotype analysis; transient luciferase assays; overexpression in wheat; RT-qPCR; detached leaf water-loss assay; infrared thermography; GC-MS wax profiling; NIL validation; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes favorable cuticular wax composition, reduces water loss, and increases drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaKCS3 was prioritized by integrated GWAS, TWAS, eQTL and network analyses as a drought-tolerance gene. 2. Higher TaKCS3 expression was positively associated with seedling survival rate. 3. Overexpression of TaKCS3 in multiple wheat cultivars increased drought survival, reduced leaf water loss, and increased leaf temperature under drought. 4. TaKCS3 altered wax composition, increasing alkane and fatty acid fractions including C31/C33 alkanes and C28 fatty acids. 5. A 369-bp promoter insertion enhanced TaKCS3 transcription and was associated with superior drought tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favorable TaKCS3 In-686 allele is a useful marker for breeding drought-tolerant wheat; overexpression or promoter editing to increase TaKCS3 expression is a promising engineering strategy
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty under well-watered conditions was reported; total wax amount did not significantly change although composition shifted
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing (Genome Variation Map: GVM000750); RNA-seq (Genome Sequence Archive: CRA016347); GWAS; TWAS; eQTL mapping
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The focal effect is on wax composition rather than total wax amount; natural promoter variation, not coding variation, was implicated as the main causal factor

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax components, especially alkanes and fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces non-stomatal water loss and enhances drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Wax biosynthesis via transcriptional control of TaKCS3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-associated upstream activator contributing to elevated TaKCS3 expression through the In-686 promoter variant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predicted from eQTL hotspot-associated TF set under drought-responsive network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: XGBoost prediction; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TaKCS3 expression, supporting wax-associated drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaTGA4 was identified from eQTL hotspot-associated TF analysis as a candidate upstream regulator of TaKCS3. 2. It bound the 369-bp insertion in the TaKCS3 promoter in Y1H and EMSA assays. 3. It enhanced reporter activity driven by the insertion sequence in dual-luciferase assays.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be stacked with favorable TaKCS3 promoter allele to enhance drought-responsive wax biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (CRA016347); eQTL hotspot analysis; XGBoost network prediction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its reported role in this paper is upstream promoter binding/activation of TaKCS3 rather than direct phenotyping of mutant or transgenic wheat lines

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax components, especially alkanes and fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces non-stomatal water loss and enhances drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Wax biosynthesis via transcriptional control of TaKCS3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-associated upstream activator contributing to elevated TaKCS3 expression through the In-686 promoter variant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predicted from eQTL hotspot-associated TF set under drought-responsive network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: XGBoost prediction; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TaKCS3 expression, supporting wax-associated drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaTGA10 was predicted as an upstream regulator of TaKCS3 from eQTL hotspot analysis. 2. It bound the 369-bp promoter insertion in Y1H and EMSA assays. 3. It enhanced reporter expression from the insertion-containing promoter fragment.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream activator to combine with favorable TaKCS3 allele in engineering of drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (CRA016347); eQTL hotspot analysis; XGBoost network prediction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is focused on promoter binding and transactivation of TaKCS3

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax components, especially alkanes and fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces non-stomatal water loss and enhances drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Wax biosynthesis via transcriptional control of TaKCS3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-associated upstream activator contributing to elevated TaKCS3 expression through the In-686 promoter variant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predicted from eQTL hotspot-associated TF set under drought-responsive network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: XGBoost prediction; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TaKCS3 expression, supporting wax-associated drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TabZIP63 was predicted as a hotspot-associated regulator of TaKCS3. 2. It directly bound the 369-bp promoter insertion in Y1H and EMSA assays. 3. It activated the insertion-containing TaKCS3 reporter in dual-luciferase assays.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory factor for boosting TaKCS3-mediated drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (CRA016347); eQTL hotspot analysis; XGBoost network prediction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts on the cis insertion sequence that differentiates favorable versus unfavorable TaKCS3 haplotypes

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax components, especially alkanes and fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces non-stomatal water loss and enhances drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Wax biosynthesis via transcriptional control of TaKCS3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-associated upstream activator contributing to elevated TaKCS3 expression through the In-686 promoter variant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predicted from eQTL hotspot-associated TF set under drought-responsive network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: XGBoost prediction; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TaKCS3 expression, supporting wax-associated drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaMYBR1 was identified as a candidate upstream regulator of TaKCS3. 2. It bound the MYBR-containing 369-bp promoter insertion in Y1H and EMSA assays. 3. It enhanced TaKCS3 insertion-reporter activity in dual-luciferase assays.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to enhance favorable TaKCS3 promoter output under drought
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (CRA016347); eQTL hotspot analysis; XGBoost network prediction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct evidence links it to the insertion-containing promoter fragment rather than whole-plant transgenic phenotypes
